name: Lane
description:
Composite library component bundling one work-coordination 'lane' -- the per-polecat slice of convoy-lands. Five inner components: Hook (PreCompact firer), Polecat (work-coord agent), Issue (the work bead), MR (merge result), Worktree (filesystem checkout). Internally wires the observe edges (Polecat<->Issue, MR observes Polecat) and the Hook->Polecat channel. Externally exposes the boundaries to the rest of the convoy-lands ensemble: SLING_TO_ISSUE in (Feeder slings to this lane's Issue), MERGE_READY_OUT out (Polecat tells Refinery this lane's work is ready), REFINERY_IN in (Refinery responds about this lane's MR), CLEAN_IN in (Landing cleans this lane's worktree).

Replaces the per-instance enumeration {Iss1, Pol1, Mr1, Wt1, Hk1} + {Iss2, Pol2, Mr2, Wt2, Hk2} that convoy-lands does today: each lane bundles itself, host instantiates Lane1, Lane2 and wires only the lane-level boundaries.

Note: Polecat's SLING in-port is bound to a Lane external port SLING_TO_POLECAT. The host typically wires both SLING_TO_ISSUE and SLING_TO_POLECAT to the same Feeder out-port (the channel-driven Polecat-advance path is redundant given the observe-driven path, but Polecat exposes both so the in-port must be wired to satisfy E009).

SLING_TO_ISSUE.description: Feeder slings work to this lane's Issue. Forwarded to Iss.SLING.

SLING_TO_POLECAT.description: Feeder also slings to this lane's Polecat (channel-driven advance path; redundant with observe-driven, but Polecat.SLING must be bound). Forwarded to Pol.SLING.

MERGE_READY_OUT.description: Polecat signals Refinery that this lane's work is complete. Forwarded from Pol.MERGE_READY.

REFINERY_IN.description: Refinery dispatches MR processing for this lane. Forwarded to Mr.REFINERY_IN.

CLEAN_IN.description: Landing requests this lane's worktree be cleaned. Forwarded to Wt.CLEAN.
